title: The Orbital Cleanup: Managing Space Debris
subtitle: An examination of how the growing accumulation of space junk threatens the future of global satellite infrastructure.
description: As low-Earth orbit becomes increasingly crowded, space debris poses a significant risk to modern communication and navigation systems. This series explores the technical, legal, and economic challenges of cleaning up the final frontier.

articles.label: Part 1
articles.note: Explain the historical origin of space debris and the theoretical risk of a chain reaction collision.

articles.label: Part 2
articles.note: Identify the key actors, including space agencies, private satellite constellations, and emerging space nations.

articles.label: Part 3
articles.note: Examine how anti-satellite (ASAT) tests have significantly worsened the debris problem.

articles.label: Part 4
articles.note: Discuss the impact on global GPS, telecommunications, and the insurance industry.

articles.label: Part 5
articles.note: Explore current initiatives for active debris removal (ADR) and future international policy frameworks.
